Petition To Establish Claim Of Ownership To Property For Return Of Funds Belonging To Decedents Estate And For Damages Imposition Of Constructive Trust And Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Dec-05-2022 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers, via Zoom, for Christopher Goldsmith, Administrator; David Bremer, via Zoom, for Alexis Rossi, Respondent.

Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to the instant Petition and whether Probate Code 850(A)(2)(D) applies to the facts in this matter. The court advises that it is not persuaded that Probate Code 850(A)(2)(D) applies. After additional discussion, the court advises that it intends to take the instant Petition off calendar. No objection to the court's tentative ruling is presented.

The PETITION TO ESTABLISH CLAIM OF OWNERSHIP TO PROPERTY FOR RETURN OF FUNDS BELONGING TO DECEDENTS ESTATE AND FOR DAMAGES IMPOSITION OF CONSTRUCTIVE TRUST AND ATTORNEY'S FEES is taken off calendar in open court.

Judge: Hon. Ross C. Moody. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Choi. Not Reported.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Close Of The Estate
Off Calendar Dec-29-2021 Continued To Jan-26-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The court stated that the February 2020 minutes supports that it needs to ascertain whether there needs to be a petition to close this estate for lack of assets or processing of the estate. An Order to Appear will be issued to the Administrator Christopher Goldsmith and the Attorney Peter Myers to appear on January 26, 2022 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 to explain why they failed to appear and why a petition for final distribution, or a petition for approval of report on status of administration has not been filed. Status on close of estate is also continued to that date.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Jul-10-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers appeared by telephone for Christopher Goldsmith (Petitioner).

The Court informed Mr. Myers that a waiver of notice by Ms. Brockwehl is needed. Mr. Myers stated that his Secretary used Fax & File on Friday. The Court Clerk was able to see that the waiver of notice was pending in the e-file queue. The Petition for Letters of Administration is taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Cabasares. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-25-2020 Continued To Jun-29-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Mar-04-2020 Continued To Apr-06-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Continued To Mar-04-20 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Peter Myers for Christopher Goldsmith (Personal Representative).

The Court raised the issue of having a Guardian Ad Litem. Mr. Myers stated that this is a $40,000.00/small estate, that the Decedent died during the divorce, that his Client/the Father has had custody of the two Minor Children and that there is an IRS lien of $40,000.00. Mr. Myers also stated that there is an embezzlement issue, that there are unknown creditor's claims, that they don't know if there are any other assets, that at the end of the day he will have a petition for extraordinary fees and referred to Finkbeiner 136 Cal.App.4th 1417 to support his position. In addition, Mr. Myers stated that the Children don't stand to receive at the end of the day unless they find assets, that the divorce Attorney may be in the best position to serve as the Guardian Ad Litem, that there is a relative in Canada, but she has no relationship with the Children and that there is no other logical person. The Court raised the issue of the divorce Attorney having a fiduciary duty to the Father, noted that the Decedent also had a divorce Attorney and that the Father's interest and the Minors' interests are not in line, that someone needs to be in place to make sure the Minor's interests are protected and that it needs to be an independent person. Mr. Myers stated that he can swap someone else in by way of an ex parte petition. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to March 4, 2020 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Tauscher. Not reported.

PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
Off Calendar Sep-16-2019 Continued To Oct-16-19 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Lily Harris for Christopher Goldsmith.

The Court raised the issue of the need to get a Guardian Ad Litem ("GAL") for the Minors. Ms. Harris asked if a GAL is still needed if bond is waived. The Court stated that a GAL is needed regardless. Ms. Harris stated that she imagines this is going to be an insolvent estate and that nothing is going to the Minors, that she believes something regarding a dissolution was pending, but nothing was finalized and that her Client believes the largest Creditor will be the Internal Revenue Service. The Petition for Letters of Administration is continued to October 16, 2019 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: C. Kabasares. Not reported.
